Class User

java.lang.Object
Auth.User
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class User extends Object implements Serializable
Represents a registered user in the Personal Budgeting System.

Stores the user's credentials and account information. Implements Serializable to allow persistence via DataManager.

See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • User

      public User(String fullName, String email, String password)
      Constructs a new User with an auto-incremented ID.
      Parameters:
      fullName - the user's full name
      email - the user's email address
      password - the user's password

  • Method Details

    • register

      public boolean register()
      Validates the user's registration data.

      Checks that the full name, email (must contain @), and password are all non-blank.

      Returns:
      true if all fields are valid; false otherwise

    • login

      public boolean login(String inputEmail, String inputPassword)
      Authenticates the user against the provided credentials.
      Parameters:
      inputEmail - the email address entered by the user
      inputPassword - the password entered by the user
      Returns:
      true if both email and password match; false otherwise

    • logout

      public void logout()
      Logs the user out of the current session.

      Session cleanup is handled by the calling UI layer.

    • getUserID

      public int getUserID()
      Returns:
      the unique user ID

    • getFullName

      public String getFullName()
      Returns:
      the user's full name

    • getEmail

      public String getEmail()
      Returns:
      the user's email address

    • getPassword

      public String getPassword()
      Returns:
      the user's password

    • getCreatedAt

      public LocalDateTime getCreatedAt()
      Returns:
      the timestamp when this account was created

    • setFullName

      public void setFullName(String n)
      Parameters:
      n - the new full name

    • setEmail

      public void setEmail(String e)
      Parameters:
      e - the new email address

    • setPassword

      public void setPassword(String p)
      Parameters:
      p - the new password
